context: Series B health-tech, HIPAA, GCP, 12K→200K IoT devices

decision_areas.elimination_reason_pulsar: Operational complexity too high, small GCP ecosystem, no strong use case differentiation
decision_areas.recommendation_rationale: Full GCP BAA coverage, Python ML native support, lowest operational burden, best cost profile

decision_areas.recommended: BigQuery (primary); ClickHouse Cloud as conditional hot-query tier
decision_areas.elimination_reason_druid: Extreme operational complexity, 6+ JVM service types, not GCP-native
decision_areas.elimination_reason_timescaledb: TigerData Cloud not available on GCP; scale limitations at 200K devices
decision_areas.recommendation_rationale: Serverless, GCP BAA, excellent cost profile, Storage Write API for near-realtime ingestion

decision_areas.recommended: Dagster+ (primary); Cloud Composer as fallback if HIPAA BAA unavailable from Dagster
decision_areas.elimination_reason_temporal: Designed for durable long-running stateful workflows, not data pipeline orchestration; lacks native BigQuery operators and data lineage
decision_areas.recommendation_rationale: Asset-based model provides compliance audit trail for model versioning; native BigQuery integration; excellent Python support

stack_configurations.name: Full GCP Native
stack_configurations.hipaa_coverage: Complete - all components under GCP BAA

stack_configurations.name: Performance-Optimized
stack_configurations.verdict: CONDITIONAL - use if BigQuery dashboard latency insufficient

components.analytics_db: ClickHouse Cloud (GCP) + BigQuery as system of record

stack_configurations.hipaa_coverage: Partial - requires ClickHouse Enterprise BAA negotiation

stack_configurations.name: Kafka-Centric (Revised)
stack_configurations.verdict: NOT RECOMMENDED as primary - evaluated to address CTO hypothesis

components.stream_processing: Python microservices on GKE consuming from Kafka

stack_configurations.hipaa_coverage: Requires Confluent Cloud BAA (available) + GCP BAA

cto_hypothesis.verdict: PARTIALLY VALID - Kafka as transport is reasonable; Kafka Streams as processor is impossible in Python
cto_hypothesis.critical_finding: Kafka Streams is a Java/Scala library with no Python API. Python ML models cannot run natively in Kafka Streams.
cto_hypothesis.valid_parts: Kafka API compatibility is real; ecosystem is rich; portability argument has merit
cto_hypothesis.invalid_parts: Python ML integration requires external services, adding latency and complexity; cost is 10x higher than Pub/Sub at target scale; GCP committed spend does not cover Confluent

data_gaps: Redpanda Cloud HIPAA BAA availability not publicly confirmed - requires direct vendor contact
Dagster+ HIPAA BAA availability not explicitly documented - requires sales contact
Confluent Cloud specific pricing for target throughput not available (dynamic pricing)
Google Cloud Composer specific pricing not extracted (dynamic JavaScript pricing page)
Redpanda BYOC GCP region availability not confirmed
confidence_level: HIGH for Stack A recommendation; MEDIUM for cost estimates at 200K device scale
recommended_next_steps: Sign Google Cloud BAA if not already executed
Contact Dagster sales to confirm HIPAA BAA availability; if unavailable, fall back to Cloud Composer
Prototype MQTT-to-Pub/Sub bridge to validate Mosquitto integration approach
Run Pub/Sub + Dataflow latency benchmark at 12K device message rate to validate <3s SLA
Set up BigQuery dataset with time-based partitioning and device_id clustering for cost efficiency
